word_list.word: the best of friends must part (ways|)
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈɛst ʌv fɹˈɛndz mˈʌst pˈɑːɹt wˈeɪz

senses.definition: used to encourage people to appreciate their friendships while recognizing the inevitability of change and separation, as any friendship may come to an end due to various circumstances

examples.x: Sarah and Emily have been inseparable since they were kids, but now that Sarah is moving across the country for college, they both know that even the best of friends must part.

senses.definition: used to imply that history can be a predictor of future events, and that human behavior and societal patterns tend to repeat themselves, emphasizes the importance of learning from the past

examples.x: The rise of extremist groups in recent years is a clear indicator of the fact that history repeats itself when we don't learn from our mistakes.

word_list.word: a man can die but once
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈæn kæn dˈaɪ bˌʌt wˈʌns

senses.definition: used to  emphasize the importance of living life to the fullest without fear of death, as it is an inevitable event that cannot be avoided

examples.x: He was afraid to stand up to his boss, but I reminded him that a man can die but once.

word_list.word: accidents will happen in the best of families
word_list.pronunciation: ˈæksɪdənts wɪl hˈæpən ɪnðə bˈɛst ʌv fˈæmɪlɪz

senses.definition: used to imply that even the most careful or well-prepared people or families can still experience unexpected mishaps or mistakes

examples.x: They were upset about the spilled wine on the carpet but remembered accidents will happen in the best of families.
Accidents will happen in the best of families, but that doesn’t mean we can’t learn from them.
He was embarrassed when his presentation didn't go well, but his colleague reassured him, accidents will happen in the best of families.
Accidents will happen in the best of families, so we shouldn’t let this little mistake ruin our day.
It was a small mistake, and he reminded himself, accidents will happen in the best of families.

senses.definition: used to say that all the ways of doing something have the same result

examples.x: My teacher always emphasized that all roads lead to Rome, and that there are many ways to approach a problem or challenge, but ultimately, the goal is to find a solution that works best for you.

word_list.word: there are (many|multiple) paths to the top of the mountain, but the view is (always|) the same
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹˌɑːɹ mˈɛni mˈʌltɪpəl pˈæθs tə ðə tˈɑːp ʌvðə mˈaʊntɪn bˌʌt ðə vjˈuː ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ðə sˈeɪm

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that regardless of the different routes taken, the ultimate outcome or result is predetermined and inevitable

word_list.word: the tongue always returns to the sore tooth
word_list.pronunciation: ðə tˈʌŋ ˈɔːlweɪz ɹɪtˈɜːnz tə ðə sˈoːɹ tˈuːθ

senses.definition: used to imply that people have a tendency to dwell on things that cause them pain or discomfort, even if doing so only makes the situation worse

examples.x: Despite her best efforts, Sarah couldn't stop thinking about the argument she had with her friend, as the tongue always returns to the sore tooth.

senses.definition: used to suggest that some things are out of human control and that people should not waste their time and energy worrying about things they cannot change

word_list.word: at the end of the game, the king and the pawn go back in the same box
word_list.pronunciation: æt ðɪ ˈɛnd ʌvðə ɡˈeɪm ðə kˈɪŋ ænd ðə pˈɔːn ɡˌoʊ bˈæk ɪnðə sˈeɪm bˈɑːks

senses.definition: used to highlight the concept that regardless of one's status or position in life, everyone ultimately faces the same fate of death
used to emphasize the importance of adaptability and flexibility, as change is a natural part of life and can often lead to new opportunities and growth

examples.x: When faced with a challenging project at work, Sarah remembered that the sea refuses no river and approached the task with an open mind.
